transcript.text: Okay, so this is my philosophy with Kyle mission statement video.
So, I'm going to start making videos of myself philosophizing,
Answering all of the biggest philosophical issues and political issues
In an attempt to help clarify with myself what I believe about everything
And share it with the world because I think everybody should do that.
And maybe I could even get into some philosophical debates with people
Who don't believe what I believe.
Like my first one, I'm going to do reincarnation because that's the easiest for me.
And maybe somebody could make a video where they bring up all the points I make
And then try to give a better reason why they don't believe in reincarnation.
And then I could make a video asking them to clarify all the points they make
And we could go back and forth.
And I think it's pretty cool to do it on YouTube
Because you get just as much time as you want to talk.
Because a lot of times when people get in these arguments,
They interrupt each other.
But when you have a YouTube video, you can't interrupt.
And I think 10 minutes is enough for each person to put across their ideas about anything
And anything that somebody else might have said about what they said.
And then other people can comment on it.
Isn't that brilliant?
And then I think my prediction is that that's the way that the Internet is going.
I think people will eventually become bored with just doing the same stuff that we do,
Just judging each other, being passive, basically, reading the news instead of being a participant
And going after truth.
And so I think people will eventually start going after truth.
And the best way to find the truth is to argue.
And to not just argue, but to even bolster each other's ideas.
Agree with somebody and then give them even more ideas.
And I think eventually if people argue with each other and discuss with each other long enough,
The truth shall eventually be revealed.
We will find the answer to everything.
And I think that we all should have a common philosophical idea.
Some people might think, oh, the world would be too boring if everybody's the same.
There should be different groups of people who believe their own special thing, special idea.
But I think the more, I think the world, the more we discuss, the more we will be individuals.
And the individuality will always grow and grow.
It will just grow in a way that we can all understand each other and we all understand ourselves.
Because, you know, the more I would argue with somebody who believes the exact opposite as I do
And they're very intelligent, then the more I have to strive to know what I actually do believe.
Because maybe I'm wrong in a lot of things.
And the only way for me to really find that out is to argue with somebody smarter than me
Who doesn't agree with what I agree with.
So, the truth shall be revealed.
And the best way to do it is with the YouTube videos.
Because you bring yourself into the video.
So, it's not just sterile.
A lot of people think that the internet revolution is bad because it makes us all,
It takes us away from each other.
We're all anonymous and our bark-a-loungers just typing away and we can say whatever we want.

Just to, you know, listen to somebody's video and then write down everything they say and then try to go against it.
